title: shawia axillaris
intro: Shawia axillaris, also known as the African Boxwood, is a plant species that belongs to the family Celastraceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin problems. The health benefits of Shawia axillaris are attributed to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing, reduce pain and swelling, and combat infections. Herbal preparations of Shawia axillaris are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are used both internally and topically to harness its medicinal benefits.
